title: Lisas Pony

jokes.text: I called her. She's not home. I also tried Mr. Flanders, Aunt Patty, Aunt Selma, Dr. Hibbert, Reverend Lovejoy... and that nice man who caught the snake in our basement.
jokes.setup: Lisa needs help finding a saxophone reed and calls various people
jokes.punchline: The escalating list ending with 'that nice man who caught the snake' shows how desperate she is
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from family to authorities to random snake catcher. Shows Lisa's methodical desperation and Homer as absolute last resort.

jokes.text: Wow. And after them... out of all the people in the world... you chose me.
jokes.setup: Lisa has called everyone else first before Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock-honored reaction to being dead last choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - simultaneously hurt and oblivious to why he'd be last choice. The pause sells it.

jokes.text: Let me caution the people in the first five rows-- You will get wet.
jokes.setup: Standard school talent show announcement
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a dangerous SeaWorld show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - treating school talent show with SeaWorld-level danger warnings. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: The doors are now locked, so you parents can't sneak out after your own child has performed.
jokes.setup: School talent show announcement
jokes.punchline: Cynical acknowledgment that parents only care about their own kid's performance
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about parent behavior at school events, delivered with Skinner's typical authority

jokes.text: Just in the nick of-- Mmm. Beer.
jokes.setup: Homer appears to be rushing to help Lisa
jokes.punchline: Gets immediately distracted by beer
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer - the iconic beer distraction derailing his good intentions. Simple but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: This is a whole lot of nothing. I'd rather watch the boilers.
jokes.setup: Homer watching kids perform at talent show
jokes.punchline: Prefers watching industrial equipment to children's performances
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of appreciation for childhood wonder, preferring literal machinery. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: D'oh! D'oh! D'oh! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer discovers the music store is closed
jokes.punchline: Multiple frustrated D'oh's as reality hits
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase usage. Repetition builds the frustration. Iconic but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: The moron next door closed early. / I happen to be that moron. / Oh, me and my trenchant mouth!
jokes.setup: Homer complains about 'the moron next door'
jokes.punchline: Store owner reveals he IS that moron, Homer's vocabulary pretension
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Homer's accidental self-insult plus 'trenchant mouth' malapropism. Well-constructed joke.

jokes.text: I'd like to open with my impression of... Principal Skinner.
jokes.setup: Bart announcing his talent show act
jokes.punchline: Choosing to impersonate the authority figure right in front of him
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart boldness - mocking authority to their face. Sets up the following jokes perfectly.

jokes.text: Duh, look at me. I'm Principal Skinner.
jokes.setup: Bart doing his Skinner impression
jokes.punchline: Crude 'duh' impression right in front of Skinner
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Bart's complete lack of respect for authority. The 'duh' makes it delightfully juvenile.

jokes.text: That young man just became... the boy of a thousand days detention.
jokes.setup: Bart was introduced as 'boy of a thousand voices'
jokes.punchline: Skinner transforms it to 'thousand days detention'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure - transforms the introduction into punishment. Perfect Skinner response with great wordplay.

jokes.text: I wonder what lunch lady Doris has for us today. / Today's special is refried... dog poop.
jokes.setup: Bart impersonating lunch lady Doris
jokes.punchline: Crude cafeteria food joke
jokes.explanation: Standard cafeteria food joke - fits Bart's crude humor but not particularly clever. Gets the job done.

jokes.text: Let me think about it. Uh... no.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately pleading to open the store
jokes.punchline: Mock consideration followed by flat rejection
jokes.explanation: Good timing and deadpan delivery, though fairly standard rejection humor

jokes.text: Come on,Jer. Open up. I pulled you out of that burning car.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince music store owner to open
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions saving his life as leverage
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual mention of heroic deed as bargaining chip. Good escalation and character logic.

jokes.text: What instrument does she play? / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately needs a reed for his daughter
jokes.punchline: Doesn't even know what instrument she plays
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Homer's oblivious parenting - desperate to help but fundamentally disconnected.

jokes.text: Lisa, stop playing that stupid-- Saxophone! That's it!
jokes.setup: Homer at home still trying to figure out Lisa's instrument
jokes.punchline: Insults the saxophone while Lisa plays it, then has eureka moment
jokes.explanation: Great structure - Homer inadvertently insulting Lisa's passion before realizing it's the answer. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Alto or tenor? / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Owner asks about saxophone type
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes he still doesn't know enough
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - just when Homer thinks he's figured it out, another layer of complexity. Classic frustration.

jokes.text: I'd like to put this filth behind us... and let's all enjoy... Lisa Simpson's rendition... of 'Stormy Leather.' / Uh, 'Weather.'
jokes.setup: Skinner introducing Lisa's performance
jokes.punchline: Misreads 'Stormy Weather' as 'Stormy Leather', gets corrected
jokes.explanation: Simple misreading joke - creates mild awkwardness but nothing special. Functional setup for Lisa's performance.

jokes.text: Sounds like that gopher I caught in me lawn mower.
jokes.setup: Lisa playing saxophone with broken reed
jokes.punchline: Willie's crude comparison to animal violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie - graphic, working-class comparison delivered in his accent. Good character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Ew, I'd hate to be that kid's father.
jokes.setup: Lisa's terrible saxophone performance
jokes.punchline: Audience member's comment, unaware Homer is right there
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's embarrassment nicely. Simple but effective dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer hears audience member's comment about Lisa's father
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to being publicly shamed
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Homer's awareness of his failure. Minimal but appropriate.

jokes.text: Let's hear it for Lisa Simpson... and her wacky sax. / Yeah! Whoo! / Must be her father.
jokes.setup: Skinner's lukewarm introduction, Homer cheers enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Audience member immediately identifies the overeager parent
jokes.explanation: Good observation about parental behavior - only the parent would cheer that enthusiastically for a bad performance.

jokes.text: Oh! That cost $88! / I don't feel much like eating.
jokes.setup: Homer orders expensive dessert for Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa loses appetite when she learns the cost
jokes.explanation: Realistic child reaction - guilt over expensive gesture killing appetite. Good character moment for Lisa.

jokes.text: I forgive you. / D'oh! You didn't mean that. / No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Lisa says she forgives Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer correctly identifies her insincerity, Lisa confirms
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest family dynamic - both characters acknowledge the lie. Great structure and realistic relationship.

jokes.text: You're taping it? / Yes. / I'll watch it later.
jokes.setup: Marge filming Lisa's first steps
jokes.punchline: Homer can't even be bothered to watch his daughter's milestone live
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of modern parenting priorities - recording over experiencing. Homer's detachment is sadly realistic.

jokes.text: No wonder she hates me. Oh, I never even noticed she was alive.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing his neglect of Lisa
jokes.punchline: Admits he barely acknowledged her existence
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest moment - Homer's complete parental failure stated bluntly. Uncomfortable truth as comedy.

jokes.text: Maybe I should give up on Lisa... and make a fresh start with Maggie.
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with father-daughter relationship
jokes.punchline: Considers abandoning Lisa for easier infant relationship
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny parenting logic - treating children like failed investments. Homer's terrible problem-solving on display.

jokes.text: If I spend any more time doing girl things... I'm going to, you know, go fruity.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about spending time with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Fears femininity will somehow affect his masculinity
jokes.explanation: Dated but characteristic Homer - fragile masculinity preventing proper parenting. Shows his limitations.

jokes.text: With today's gasoline prices, we can't afford not to.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying buying a pony
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical economic logic about gas prices
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic - connecting unrelated economic concepts. Perfectly encapsulates his reasoning style.

jokes.text: When you yell at me, I see love in your eyes.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his purchase decision to angry Marge
jokes.punchline: Interprets her anger as love
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional relationship interpretation. Funny but reveals deeper character dysfunction.

jokes.text: Was that a yes or a no? / Baa. / Those aren't even words. / Sna.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to get straight answer from Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer devolves into nonsensical animal sounds
jokes.explanation: Homer's regression to non-language to avoid commitment. Good escalation from evasion to complete nonsense.

jokes.text: 'Scottish Deer Hound.' Hey, this is a dog! / My friend, you're smarter than I gave you credit for.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at 'ponies' in pet store
jokes.punchline: Store owner's backhanded compliment when Homer notices it's a dog
jokes.explanation: Good con-man character work - insulting Homer while appearing to compliment him. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: That stunning creature over there... is half a million dollars. / Half a million dollars?! / He was sired by Seattle Slew. His mother won the Kentucky Derby. His likeness graces a stamp in Tanzania.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to buy expensive pony
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd credentials ending with Tanzania stamp
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from legitimate horse racing pedigree to completely random Tanzania stamp. Good absurd comedy.

jokes.text: This check is dated January 1, 2054. / Is there a problem with that?
jokes.setup: Homer writes check for future date
jokes.punchline: Genuinely confused why that would be problematic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer financial logic - post-dating by 63 years seems reasonable to him. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Isn't there like a pound... where you can pick up cheap ponies... that ran away from home?
jokes.setup: Homer can't afford expensive ponies
jokes.punchline: Thinks there's a pony equivalent of animal shelters for runaways
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly naive Homer logic - applying pet shelter concept to expensive horses. Creative and character-specific.

jokes.text: Are you acquainted with our state's stringent usury laws? / Usury? / Silly me. I made up a word that doesn't exist.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions usury laws to Homer
jokes.punchline: Pretends he invented the word when Homer doesn't know it
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns manipulation - pretending legitimate legal term is made-up to exploit Homer's ignorance. Great character work.

jokes.text: You're not planning to eat it? / It's for my little girl because she doesn't love me.
jokes.setup: Burns asks about Homer's pony plans
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutally honest emotional reason
jokes.explanation: Homer's painfully honest admission cuts through Burns' casual question. Good contrast in character styles.

jokes.text: Shut up, Simpson. / Smithers, let's not be so cold. His spirit is my collateral.
jokes.setup: Burns being harsh to Homer
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to fake compassion while claiming Homer's soul
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - switching from cruelty to fake benevolence while making Faustian deal. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: I didn't do anything funny, sir. / Shut up.
jokes.setup: Burns claims Smithers did something funny earlier
jokes.punchline: Smithers' literal denial followed by Burns' dismissal
jokes.explanation: Standard Burns-Smithers dynamic - Burns' casual cruelty and Smithers' literal responses. Functional character work.

jokes.text: Sounds like someone's angling... for a pony of her own.
jokes.setup: Marge is upset about the pony purchase
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets her anger as jealousy
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete misreading of situation - thinks adult woman wants pony. Good character obliviousness.

jokes.text: I know you love me, so you don't get squat.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why Lisa gets a pony
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards reward system - no gifts for loved ones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inverted parenting logic - punishing love, rewarding indifference. Perfectly twisted Homer reasoning.

jokes.text: This is Princess. Please don't exclude her because she's different.
jokes.setup: Introducing the pony to other pets
jokes.punchline: Treating size difference as discrimination issue
jokes.explanation: Homer applying social justice language to obviously impractical pet situation

jokes.text: By day, it will roam free around the neighborhood... and at night, it will nestle snugly... between the cars in our garage.
jokes.setup: Marge asks where they'll keep the pony
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely impractical living arrangement plan
jokes.explanation: Absurd Homer planning - treats horse like outdoor cat. The detailed visualization makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's illegal. / That's for the courts to decide.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out pony plan is illegal
jokes.punchline: Homer defers obvious legal question to judiciary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer legal logic - obviously illegal act becomes constitutional question. Great economy and character.

jokes.text: Wait! Where's the hyperspace? / Grandpa, you're the spaceship. / I thought I was this guy.
jokes.setup: Grandpa playing video game with Bart
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion about his role in the game
jokes.explanation: Good generational gap humor - elderly confusion with video game concepts. Realistic but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I got down on the floor for this?!
jokes.setup: Video game ends quickly
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's indignation at physical effort wasted
jokes.explanation: Realistic elderly complaint - minor physical effort feels wasted. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: We're always buying Maggie... vaccinations for diseases she doesn't even have.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for expenses to cut
jokes.punchline: Treating preventive medicine as wasteful spending
jokes.explanation: Homer's medical ignorance - doesn't understand prevention concept. Good character-specific stupidity.

jokes.text: We could cut down on beer. / We're not going to do that.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests reducing beer consumption
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately dismisses only practical suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities - beer is non-negotiable expense. Simple but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Homer... what has reduced you to such cheap chicanery?
jokes.setup: Homer's obvious lottery ticket scam
jokes.punchline: Apu's eloquent disappointment in Homer's pathetic scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Apu vocabulary - sophisticated language for petty crime disappointment

jokes.text: Three liberty bells! / That will be $10,000, Apu. / If I could see the ticket-- / There it is. / Please to be removing your thumb.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he won lottery scratch-off
jokes.punchline: Covering losing symbols with thumb to fake winning
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - obvious cheating attempt. Well-structured reveal of Homer's desperation.

jokes.text: If you need money... you should have jammed a gun in my ribs... or inquire about my help wanted sign.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for money through fake lottery win
jokes.punchline: Apu suggests robbery or employment as better options
jokes.explanation: Excellent Apu logic - robbery is more honest than fraud. Great character-specific reasoning.

jokes.text: These hot dogs have been here for three years. They are strictly ornamental. There is only one bozo who comes in and buys them. / But I eat-- / Oh.
jokes.setup: Apu explaining store policies to new employee Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer realizes he's the 'bozo' customer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup-payoff - Apu unknowingly insulting his new employee. Perfect character intersection and revelation.

jokes.text: Here's a pointer. Try taking it in the shoulder.
jokes.setup: Apu warning Homer about being shot at on the job
jokes.punchline: Casual advice about optimal bullet placement
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Apu treating gunshot wounds like a technical skill with helpful tips

jokes.text: Oh, my God, she killed him.
jokes.setup: Homer sees Lisa lying on the pony
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets peaceful scene as death
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic misinterpretation of tender moment. Simple visual gag with character-appropriate overreaction.

jokes.text: While I'm here with you... my store is going down the tubes.
jokes.setup: Apu trying to enjoy Homer's company
jokes.punchline: Work anxiety preventing him from relaxing
jokes.explanation: Realistic workaholic anxiety - can't enjoy leisure time. Good character consistency for Apu.

jokes.text: 30 years. / D'oh.
jokes.setup: Homer asks how long horses live
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's committed to decades of expense
jokes.explanation: Homer's dawning realization of long-term consequences. Simple but effective buyer's remorse.

jokes.text: I heard Milli Vanilli was arrested... for impersonating a McNugget.
jokes.setup: Homer making small talk while exhausted
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled pop culture reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd misremembering - combines lip-syncing scandal with McDonald's. Sleep deprivation comedy gold.

jokes.text: Although there is no change in my patrician facade... my heart is breaking.
jokes.setup: Lisa giving up her beloved pony
jokes.punchline: Stable owner's overly formal emotional description
jokes.explanation: Perfect aristocratic character voice - formal language for simple emotion. Good contrast with genuine sentiment.

jokes.text: The young man you replaced is rolling over in his grave.
jokes.setup: Apu criticizing Homer's work performance
jokes.punchline: Implies previous employee died from job stress
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor - treating convenience store job as life-threatening. Good Apu exaggeration.

jokes.text: There's a big dumb animal... I love more than that horse. / What is it, a hippopotamus?
jokes.setup: Lisa describing someone she loves more than her pony
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of 'big dumb animal'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - can't recognize he's being lovingly insulted. Sweet moment with great misdirection.

jokes.text: He slept, he stole... he was rude to the customers. Still, there goes the best damned employee... a convenience store ever had.
jokes.setup: Apu listing Homer's terrible work habits
jokes.punchline: Still considers him the best employee ever
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction - terrible employee qualities somehow equal excellence. Perfect Apu logic and great episode ending.

tmdb_overview: After disappointing Lisa, Homer buys her a pony, solving one problem, but creating others, requiring a second job--at Apu's Kwik-E-Mart.

guest_stars.name: Pamela Hayden
guest_stars.character: Milhouse Van Houten / Credit Union Clerk / Kid (voice)

insight: Sixty-one jokes in 22 minutes, but emotional core undermines laugh momentum.
